title: Action Menu
subtitle: Dropdown menu component with support for static, dynamic items and submenus

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple action menu with an array of items. Each item can have a label, icon and action.
basic.note: Here we see an example of using both type='inline' and type='dropdown'.
basic.consoleNote: Open DevTools (F12) and check the Console tab to see the actions logged when you click on menu items.

separators.title: Separators
separators.description: Group related actions using visual separators.
separators.note: Use items with the separator: true property to add dividing lines between groups of actions. You can add an optional label to the separator using the label property.

submenu.title: Submenus
submenu.description: Create nested menus using the children property. Submenus can be nested multiple levels.
submenu.note: Use the children property to define submenu items. Each child can have its own children for further nesting. Note: If a child has selected: true, the parent item will show a visual indicator (vertical line on the left) to aid navigation.

variants.title: Button variants
variants.description: The button supports different variants and colors from the NUI system. The variant affects the button, while the color affects both the color and its content.
variants.note: Combine color and variant to customize the appearance of the menu button to your needs.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Use the five predefined NUI sizes. The size affects the button, but the content will remain the same.
sizes.note: Use the size input to adjust the size of the menu button and its content using the sizes xs, sm, md, lg and xl.

label.title: Button content
label.description: Add text to the menu button. It can be combined with icons and supports full width mode.
label.note: Use icon together with label to display both in the button. Use width='full' to make the button occupy the full available width. If you only use label without an icon, the button renders as a simple text button.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Disable individual menu items or the entire menu. Disabled items are shown but are not clickable.

withSubtitle.title: Item subtitle
withSubtitle.description: Add secondary text below the main label. Useful for displaying additional information such as emails, descriptions or shortcuts.

selected.title: Selected state
selected.description: Mark items as selected to show the current choice. A check icon appears automatically. Perfect for selectors, filters or settings.

icons.title: Descriptive icons
icons.description: Use meaningful icons and labels to communicate the purpose of each action. Icons help users quickly identify actions.
icons.note: Use relevant icons from the Remix Icon library to improve visual understanding of menu actions.

badge.title: Badge
badge.description: Display a counter or numeric label on menu items using the badge property. Ideal for notifications, pending counters or any visual indicator.
badge.note: The badge renders as a pill positioned at the far right of the item. Screen readers access the badge value through the button's aria-label (e.g. "Messages (3)"), while the visual pill is hidden with aria-hidden.

template.title: Custom template
template.description: Fully customize the rendering of the menu using templates and directives.
template.note:
The #item template receives the full item as context (let-item). The menu-header and menu-footer directives allow a simple and clear syntax, avoiding unnecessary ng-templates.

Important: When using custom templates, you must handle the click event manually with (click)="handleAction(item)". The onItemAction event is only emitted automatically with default items.

inputs.title: Component inputs
inputs.description: Input properties that modify the behavior and appearance of the action menu.

offset.description: Distance in pixels between the trigger and the floating menu panel (dynamic mode only)

offsetSubmenu.description: Distance in pixels between a parent item and its nested submenu panel

outputs.title: Component outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the component that you can listen to in order to react to user interactions.

onItemAction.description: Emitted when an item is clicked

onSelectionChange.description: Emitted when the selection changes (selector mode)

interface.title: ActionMenuItem Interface
interface.description: Full structure of a menu item with all available properties.

selected.description: Whether the item is selected (shows checkmark)

separator.description: Whether it is a visual separator

onAction.description: Callback when the item is clicked

data.description: Additional custom data for use with TemplateRef<any>

structure.title: Structural variables
structure.description: CSS variables that control dimensions, spacing, borders and shadows of the menu.

items.title: Item variables
items.description: CSS variables to customize the appearance of items, separators, shortcuts and internal elements.

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variable pattern to customize menu item colors by state.
colors.note: Replace {color} with any NUI semantic color (primary, secondary, accent, success, info, warning, danger, neutral) to customize item appearance in different states.

styling.title: Styles and customization
styling.description: Customize the appearance of the menu using CSS variables. Control colors, spacing, typography and more.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The Action Menu applies the following ARIA attributes following the menu pattern from the ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG).

haspopup.description: Announces to assistive technologies that activating this button will open a menu.

expanded.description: Reflects the open/closed state of the menu. Updated automatically on every interaction.

controls.description: Links the trigger button to the menu container it controls via matching IDs.

roleMenu.description: Identifies the dropdown container as a menu widget.

orientation.description: Specifies that items are arranged vertically (default menu layout).

labelledby.description: In dynamic mode, links the menu container to its trigger button providing an accessible name.

roleItem.description: Default role for interactive items. Changes to menuitemradio when the item belongs to a selection group.

ariaChecked.description: Present only in selection mode. Reflects whether this item is the current selection.

keyshortcuts.description: Exposes the keyboard shortcut text displayed visually alongside the item.

roleSeparator.description: Identifies the visual divider between groups of menu items.

ariaHiddenIcon.description: Prevents decorative icons from being announced by screen readers.

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The trigger button resolves its accessible name following a priority cascade. Always provide an explicit accessible name for trigger buttons that only show an icon.
naming.note: Icon-only menus (without a label) must have ariaLabel or title configured. Without a visible text label, assistive technologies cannot identify the purpose of the menu.

triggerLabel.description: The label input renders as the button's inner text, which natively becomes the accessible name.

triggerAriaLabel.description: The ariaLabel input is applied as the aria-label attribute and takes priority over label and title.

triggerIconOnly.description: If neither ariaLabel nor label is provided, the title input is used as the accessible name via the browser tooltip.

itemLabel.description: The aria-label of each menu item is resolved from item.title || item.label to ensure screen readers announce the correct text.

menuLabelledby.description: In dynamic mode, the menu container references the trigger's ID via aria-labelledby, making their association explicit.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: The Action Menu implements the full APG menu keyboard pattern. Arrow keys navigate between items; Escape and ArrowLeft close menus.
keyboard.note: ArrowDown, ArrowUp, Home and End call preventDefault() to prevent page scrolling while navigating the menu.

tab.description: Moves focus to the trigger button. When the menu is open, Tab closes it and returns focus to the trigger.

spaceEnter.description: Opens the menu when focus is on the trigger. Activates the focused menu item when inside the menu.

escape.description: Closes the open menu and returns focus to the trigger button.

arrowDown.description: Moves focus to the next menu item. Wraps from the last item to the first.

arrowUp.description: Moves focus to the previous menu item. Wraps from the first item to the last.

home.description: Moves focus to the first menu item.

end.description: Moves focus to the last menu item.

arrowRight.description: Opens the submenu of the focused item and moves focus to its first item.

arrowLeft.description: Closes the current submenu and returns focus to the parent item.

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: Set default values for all Action Menu instances in your application using the provideNUI() function. Any property defined here is automatically applied to every action menu that does not override it via a direct input binding.
setup.note: Only the properties you want to override need to be defined. Properties not specified fall back to the library defaults.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: Full list of configurable properties through ActionMenuGlobalConfig, their types and the library defaults.

rows.color: Color theme applied to all action menus by default
rows.size: Size applied to all action menus by default
rows.variant: Visual variant applied to all action menus by default
rows.offset: Distance in pixels between the trigger and the menu panel
rows.offsetSubmenu: Distance in pixels between a parent item and its nested submenu panel

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: Values are resolved following a strict three-level priority chain. The first matching level wins: direct input > global config > library default.
priority.note: The priority chain guarantees that components always have a valid resolved value. A direct input always wins; if not provided, the global config value is used; if not configured either, the library default is applied.
